title: Broadcast lock-free design: collect channels before awaiting is correct
description: state.rs broadcast() collects all ClientTx clones into a Vec before awaiting any sends, releasing the DashMap lock before any async work. A contributor might 'simplify' this to iterate DashMap and await inside the iterator, but that would hold a DashMap shard lock across an await point — a deadlock if any send path tries to acquire the same shard. The current pattern is the correct idiom for async fan-out over a DashMap. Why it should be defended: holding DashMap shard locks across await is undefined behavior (deadlock) in this design.
close_reason: decision already recorded in CLAUDE.md / architecture is intentional; no code change needed
